description: Document structure template for engage-prism analysis proposal generation (Step 5)

sections.heading: ## Research Plan
sections.content: Entity anchors, source classes, recency window, authority preferences, and fetch budget from Step 0. If webResearchPerformed=false, this section includes the degradation reason and the implication for the recommendation.

sections.heading: ## Signal Analysis
sections.content: Matched signals with weights; loaded paradigms/structures/strategies with IDs and scores.

subsections.heading: ### Brief
subsections.content: The filled brief sent to the subagent — assigned angle, research plan, citation requirement, exclusions (if any).

subsections.heading: ### Report
subsections.content: Full structured report: core claim, webResearch status, citations[], evidenceBase, analysis steps with citation references, key numbers, counter-evidence, recommendation-in-angle, fit score.

subsections.heading: ### Citation Validation
subsections.content: Per-path findings from the citation-conformance and claim-support checks: which claims are fully supported, which are partially supported, which are unsupported.

subsections.heading: ### Scoring Matrix
subsections.content: Table of dimension scores per path (always-relevant + conditionally-relevant + operational dimensions).

subsections.heading: ### Hybridization Analysis
subsections.content: Cross-path combination opportunities — framing+evidence, base-case+tail-risk, aggregate+segment, quant+qual.

sections.heading: ## Recommendation
sections.content: Final recommendation with rationale that references specific citations. If any path was degraded (webResearch.performed=false), the recommendation names the degradation explicitly.

sections.heading: ## What Would Change This Answer
sections.content: 2-3 specific observations — new evidence, updated input, or counterfactual — that would flip the recommendation.

sections.heading: ## Rejected Angles
sections.content: Angles considered during signal matching but not selected. Each entry: angle name, paradigm/structure/strategy, reason for rejection (too similar to selected path, low signal match score, out-of-domain for this question).
